evidence_found.locator: Recorded Statement — Aliyah re: assigned route
evidence_found.text_excerpt: Yes ma'am. Causeway is the route back to the terminal. There's not really another way to get there from the Adamo warehouse, unless you wanted to go all the way around on the interstate, which, you know, nobody does.
Empty. Or, uh, mostly empty. I had a couple of, uh, empty pallets in the back from one of the stops. That was it.

16:42   INCIDENT REPORTED — Causeway Blvd / Bermuda Ave intersection. Driver radioed terminal at 16:44. PD dispatched. Tow scheduled 17:12. Truck inbound to terminal at time of incident; ETA to terminal was 17:00 per route plan. Post-trip inspection / unit lock-in not yet performed (incident occurred prior to scheduled check-in).

Confirmation that no adverse MVR entries exist and the full CA 99 03 schedule contains no additional operator restrictions would further consolidate mass toward clean coverage.

coverage_analysis_memo.body:
COVERAGE ANALYSIS MEMORANDUM

File No.:        CLM-2023-04471
Exposure:        EXP-2023-04471-REYES-BI
Claimant:        Janet M. Reyes (PTY-CLM-REYES)
Named Insured:   Northwind Logistics, LLC
Policy:          NWL-CA-024471 (CA 00 01 ISO Commercial Auto)
Coverage Part:   CP-NWL-2023-BI — Auto Bodily Injury Liability
Date of Loss:    03/13/2023
Prepared:        2025-01-30

─────────────────────────────────────────────
I. COVERAGE TRIGGER ANALYSIS
─────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Policy In Force / Loss Date
The declarations page (DOC-001) confirms the policy period as 01/01/2023 12:01 A.M. to 01/01/2024 12:01 A.M. The police report (DOC-002) and dispatch log (DOC-004) confirm the loss occurred on 03/13/2023 at 16:42 hours — squarely within the policy period. This element is satisfied.

2. Covered Auto
The policy is written on Symbol 7 (Specifically Described Autos) for liability. The declarations page schedules Unit 14 — the 2019 Freightliner M2-106 (VIN 1FVACWDT9KHKL4218) — with Liability, Comp, and Collision coverages. The police report confirms V-1 in this loss is the same vehicle, owned by Northwind Logistics, insured under policy NWL-CA-024471. This element is satisfied.

3. Authorized Driver / Driver Eligibility
The dispatch log (DOC-004) identifies Marcus Aliyah (CDL #A2241-FL, exp. 2024-08-31) as the assigned driver of Unit 14 for the BR-Mon-A route on 03/13/2023 with no reassignment or swap noted. His CDL was valid as of the loss date. The police report (DOC-002) confirms Aliyah's license as FL CDL A2241. The declarations page (DOC-001) requires a valid CDL Class B; a Class A CDL (held by Aliyah) subsumes Class B privileges. 

OPEN ITEM: The MVR itself is not among the documents reviewed. The declarations page requires an annual MVR pull by the producer. The file should be supplemented with the current-year MVR to confirm the absence of disqualifying entries. Until received, a nominal question mark exists on driver eligibility.

4. Scope / Course of Business Use
Aliyah's recorded statement (DOC-003) and the dispatch log (DOC-004) establish that at the time of the loss, he was returning Unit 14 to the Tampa-Causeway terminal following completion of the final delivery on the BR-Mon-A route (last drop logged 15:51 at 4471 E Adamo Dr). The dispatch log explicitly states: "Vehicle use during the return leg (Adamo → Causeway terminal) is on-duty per Northwind operations manual §4.3 (truck must be returned to terminal before driver clock-out)." The incident at 16:42 occurred before the 17:00 scheduled check-out. No deviation, personal errand, or frolic is identified in any document. This element appears satisfied on present evidence.

5. Nature of Claim / Coverage Part
Reyes is a third-party claimant alleging bodily injury (neck and lower-back pain) arising from a rear-end collision caused by the covered auto. This is a textbook third-party BI claim. The policy provides a $1,000,000 combined single limit per accident with a $0 liability deductible under CP-NWL-2023-BI. No BI-specific exclusions are implicated by the available facts.

6. Absence of Exclusionary Conduct
The police report (DOC-002) documents no alcohol, no drugs, and no distraction for V-1. The contributing factor is solely improper following distance (FSS 316.0895(1) — Following Too Closely citation issued to Aliyah). No intentional acts are alleged. No pollution or hazmat issue arises; the truck was empty of cargo at the time (DOC-003).

─────────────────────────────────────────────
II. COVERAGE GAPS AND OPEN ITEMS
─────────────────────────────────────────────

A. MVR Not in File
The declarations page requires an annual MVR pull by the producer. Aliyah's MVR has not been produced among the reviewed documents. A disqualifying entry (e.g., DUI, CDL suspension, disqualification) could implicate the driver eligibility condition. ACTION: Request current MVR from producer (Gulf Coast Commercial Brokerage).

B. Full CA 99 03 Schedule Endorsement Not Produced
The declarations page references endorsement CA 99 03 for the full schedule of covered autos; only an excerpt appears on the declarations page. If the full endorsement contains additional use restrictions, geographic limitations, or operator schedules beyond what appears in the excerpt, those provisions have not been reviewed. ACTION: Pull complete CA 99 03 endorsement from the policy file.

C. Full ISO CA 00 01 Policy Form Not Reviewed
Only the declarations page has been reviewed. The body of the ISO CA 00 01 form — including the definitions section, exclusions, and conditions — has not been produced. Analysis is based on standard ISO CA 00 01 form structure plus the declarations page contents. No non-standard endorsements that would restrict BI liability coverage are visible in the declarations.

─────────────────────────────────────────────
III. APPLICABLE LIMIT AND DEDUCTIBLE
─────────────────────────────────────────────

Coverage Part CP-NWL-2023-BI:
  Limit:      $1,000,000 CSL per accident
  Deductible: $0
  SIR:        None

This single-claimant BI exposure is subject to the full $1,000,000 limit. No deductible erodes the available indemnity.

─────────────────────────────────────────────
IV. OUTCOME DISTRIBUTION SUMMARY
─────────────────────────────────────────────

Clean Coverage:         0.91
Reservation of Rights:  0.07
Denial:                 0.02

The distribution is strongly weighted toward clean coverage on present evidence. The ROR and denial probabilities reflect open file items (MVR, full endorsement, full policy form) rather than any affirmative exclusionary fact currently established in the record.

─────────────────────────────────────────────
V. RECOMMENDED NEXT STEPS FOR ADJUSTER REVIEW
─────────────────────────────────────────────

1. Obtain Aliyah's current-year MVR from producer Gulf Coast Commercial Brokerage.
2. Pull complete CA 99 03 schedule endorsement and full ISO CA 00 01 policy form from the policy file.
3. Proceed with liability and damages investigation (medical records from Tampa General, Reyes statement, photographs of V-2 damage, repair/diminution estimates).
4. Note the CSL structure: PD and BI share the $1,000,000 per-accident limit; monitor for V-2 property damage claim (CP-NWL-2023-PD, $1,000 deductible) that may erode the same per-occurrence limit if treated as a single accident under the CSL.
5. Auto Med Pay endorsement (CA 99 03, $5,000) may respond to Reyes's medical expenses as first-party med pay is not at issue here, but note the insured's own occupants may have a separate Med Pay claim avenue.

ror_letter.body:
RESERVATION OF RIGHTS LETTER
[DRAFT — FOR ADJUSTER REVIEW ONLY — NOT FINALIZED]

[DATE]

Northwind Logistics, LLC
4218 Causeway Blvd
Tampa, FL 33619

Re:   Claim No.:        CLM-2023-04471
      Policy No.:       NWL-CA-024471
      Date of Loss:     March 13, 2023
      Claimant:         Janet M. Reyes
      Vehicle:          Unit 14 — 2019 Freightliner M2-106

Dear Northwind Logistics:

This letter is provided on behalf of [Carrier Name] ("the Company") with respect to the above-referenced claim. The Company acknowledges receipt of notice of the March 13, 2023 accident on Causeway Blvd at Bermuda Ave, Tampa, Florida, involving Unit 14 operated by Marcus Aliyah.

The Company is conducting a coverage investigation and, to the extent set forth herein, wishes to notify you that it is reserving its rights under Policy No. NWL-CA-024471 while that investigation continues. This letter does not constitute a denial of coverage. The Company will continue to investigate and will advise you promptly upon resolution of the issues identified below.

I. POLICY PROVISIONS AT ISSUE

Policy NWL-CA-024471 is a Commercial Automobile Policy issued on ISO Form CA 00 01 with a policy period of January 1, 2023 to January 1, 2024. The Declarations Page states, with respect to driver eligibility:

    "All scheduled drivers must hold a valid CDL Class B for Unit 14. Annual MVR pull
    by Producer. Drivers must be 23 years of age or older with three years
    documented commercial driving experience."

The Declarations Page further provides that liability coverage applies to Symbol 7 — Specifically Described Autos. The full schedule of covered autos and any additional conditions are set forth in endorsement CA 99 03, a complete copy of which has been requested.

II. BASIS FOR RESERVATION

The Company has identified the following open items that it is unable to resolve on the information currently available:

1. Driver Motor Vehicle Record (MVR): The policy conditions require an annual MVR pull by the producer confirming driver eligibility. The Company has not yet received Marcus Aliyah's current-year MVR. In the event the MVR discloses a disqualifying violation, license suspension, revocation, or disqualification under applicable Florida CDL standards, a question may arise as to whether Mr. Aliyah constitutes an eligible driver under the policy at the time of the loss. The Company expressly reserves its rights on this basis pending receipt and review of the MVR.

2. Full CA 99 03 Schedule Endorsement: The Declarations Page references endorsement CA 99 03 for the complete schedule of covered autos. The Company has not yet reviewed the complete endorsement. To the extent the full endorsement contains operator restrictions, geographic limitations, or use conditions beyond those visible on the Declarations Page excerpt, the Company reserves its rights with respect to any such provisions.

3. Full Policy Form: The Company's analysis to date has been based upon the Declarations Page produced in the file. The Company reserves all rights afforded under the complete ISO CA 00 01 policy form, including all definitions, exclusions, and conditions, pending completion of its review.

III. COMPANY'S ONGOING OBLIGATIONS

Notwithstanding the foregoing reservation, the Company will continue to investigate this claim, including the liability and damages aspects, and will respond promptly as additional information becomes available. Nothing in this letter should be construed as a waiver of any right, term, condition, or provision of the policy, nor as an admission of coverage or liability.

IV. REQUEST FOR COOPERATION

You are reminded of your obligation under the policy to cooperate with the Company's investigation, including by providing access to driver records and any relevant documentation. Please direct your producer, Gulf Coast Commercial Brokerage, Inc., to produce the current-year MVR for Marcus Aliyah and the complete CA 99 03 schedule endorsement at your earliest convenience.

If you have any questions, please contact the undersigned.

Sincerely,

[Claim Specialist Name]
[Title]
[Carrier Name]
[Address]
[Phone / Email]

[This is a draft document prepared for adjuster review. It is not a final coverage position and has not been issued to the insured. The adjuster retains full discretion over the coverage path and the content of any communication sent to the insured.]

Dear Northwind Logistics:

This letter is provided on behalf of [Carrier Name] ("the Company") with respect to the above-referenced claim arising from the March 13, 2023 accident on Causeway Blvd at Bermuda Ave, Tampa, Florida.

The Company has completed its coverage investigation and has determined that coverage is not available under Policy No. NWL-CA-024471 for the reasons set forth below.

[ADJUSTER — INSERT SPECIFIC EXCLUSIONARY GROUND(S) HERE. Examples, to be used only if affirmatively established by investigation:]

    "All scheduled drivers must hold a valid CDL Class B for Unit 14. Annual MVR pull
    by Producer. Drivers must be 23 years of age or older with three years
    documented commercial driving experience."

The Company's review of the Motor Vehicle Record for Marcus Aliyah, the operator of Unit 14 at the time of the loss, discloses [DESCRIBE DISQUALIFYING ENTRY — e.g., CDL suspension, revocation, disqualifying violation]. As a result, Mr. Aliyah did not qualify as an eligible driver under the terms of the policy at the time of the loss. Coverage is therefore unavailable.

[OPTION B — Use Restriction (if CA 99 03 schedule endorsement reveals applicable restriction)]
Endorsement CA 99 03 to Policy NWL-CA-024471 contains the following provision: [INSERT APPLICABLE RESTRICTION TEXT FROM ENDORSEMENT]. The facts of this loss — [DESCRIBE HOW FACTS DEVIATE FROM RESTRICTION] — fall outside the scope of covered use as defined by that provision. Accordingly, coverage is unavailable.

[STANDARD CLOSING]
The Company's determination is based upon the policy terms in effect as of the date of loss. The Company does not waive any additional grounds for denial that may come to light, and expressly reserves all rights under the policy.

You have the right to seek review of this determination. [INSERT FLORIDA-SPECIFIC DISPUTE RESOLUTION / DFS NOTICE LANGUAGE AS REQUIRED BY FLORIDA STATUTE AND DFS RULES.]

If you believe this determination was made in error, or if you have additional information you wish the Company to consider, please contact the undersigned promptly.

Sincerely,

[Claim Specialist Name]

[Address]
